skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic brand colors, typography, and visual styling guidance to documents, presentations, and other design artifacts.
skill.summary: Apply Anthropic brand colors and typography to visual artifacts.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: AI review found no executable commands, network calls, cryptographic code, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts. Static findings are false positives from Apache License text, Markdown inline color values, and brand documentation. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community provenance.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to standard Apache License reference links. They are plain legal text and do not perform network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are inside the unmodified Apache License text. No code or instruction invokes these URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings occur in license prose and brand guidance. No hash, cipher, signature, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain license wording, metadata, keywords, and font fallback prose. There is no cryptographic implementation or recommendation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Color Values and Library Reference
low_findings.description: The external command, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance findings are Markdown inline values or a python-pptx RGBColor reference. They do not execute shell commands or collect system or network data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited backticks are Markdown formatting around hex color values. The RGBColor line names a presentation color class, not a network or command operation.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent visual assets that match Anthropic brand standards. This skill applies documented colors, typography, and styling guidance to presentations, documents, and visual artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Applies Anthropic main colors for text, backgrounds, and subtle surfaces.
Uses orange, blue, and green accent colors for non-text shapes.
Maps headings to Poppins with Arial fallback.
Maps body text to Lora with Georgia fallback.
Supports smart text color selection based on background contrast.
Preserves hierarchy while updating visual formatting.
limitations: Requires target artifacts or content from the user before styling can be applied.
Does not install Poppins or Lora fonts on the system.
Does not provide a complete legal trademark review.
Does not include executable automation scripts in the skill files.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Presentation
use_cases.description: Convert a mixed-style slide deck into a cleaner presentation with Anthropic colors and typography.

use_cases.title: Format Internal Documents
use_cases.description: Apply consistent headings, body text, and accent colors to reports or planning documents.

use_cases.title: Prepare Design Drafts
use_cases.description: Create first-pass visual assets that follow the documented palette and font guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Brand Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple visual refresh.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this document or presentation with the brand-guidelines skill. Keep the content unchanged and apply the documented colors and typography.

prompt_templates.title: Style a Slide Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this slide deck and apply Anthropic brand styling. Improve headings, body text, backgrounds, and accent shapes while preserving the message.

prompt_templates.title: Create Brand-Compliant Visuals
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a new visual artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-page visual summary using the brand-guidelines skill. Use the approved palette, clear hierarchy, and readable typography.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Refine an Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this asset for brand consistency. Then revise colors, typography, contrast, and visual hierarchy using the brand-guidelines skill.

output_examples.input: A presentation uses inconsistent fonts, dark text on dark backgrounds, and unrelated accent colors.
output: Headings are updated to the recommended heading font with a readable fallback.
Body text is updated to the recommended body font with a readable fallback.
Accent shapes use the documented orange, blue, and green palette.

output_examples.input: A product brief needs Anthropic visual styling before sharing with leadership.
output: The brief uses the documented light background and dark text pairing.
Section headings have clearer hierarchy and consistent typography.
Supporting callouts use subtle gray surfaces and approved accent colors.

best_practices: Provide the artifact and explain the audience before requesting styling.
Keep source content final before applying brand formatting.
Review contrast and readability after the styling pass.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a replacement for official legal trademark review.
Do not ask it to invent new brand colors outside the documented palette.
Do not apply styling before the structure and content are stable.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It applies documented Anthropic colors, typography, and visual styling guidance to suitable artifacts.

faq.question: Does it install fonts?
faq.answer: No. It uses available system fonts and falls back to Arial or Georgia when needed.

faq.question: Can it style presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes presentation-oriented color and typography behavior, including RGB color usage.

faq.question: Can it create official brand approvals?
faq.answer: No. It helps with visual consistency but does not replace formal brand or legal approval.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no executable code, network behavior, or prompt injection attempts.
